nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes was born in 1268, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England. She married Sir John de Trafford in 1291, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes est née en 1268, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir John de Trafford en 1291, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes wurde 1268 in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat 1291 in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England Sir John de Trafford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes è nata il nel 1268 nel Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato Sir John de Trafford nel 1291, nel Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes(은)는 1268년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1291년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 Sir John de Trafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnes nasceu em 1268, em Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Trafford em 1291, em Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes родилась в 1268 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Trafford в 1291 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Agnes nació en 1268, en Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir John de Trafford en 1291, en Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas.
